channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Breaking the Source Code Barrier: IAF Moves to Independently Integrate Missiles on Su-30MKI
item.description: The Indian Air Force has initiated a crucial but largely behind-the-scenes project that could redefine how India integrates weapons onto its frontline fighter fleet. The tender for the Indigenous Development of a Weapon Integration Tester (WIT) for the Su-30MKI, identified as TID: 2026_IAF_757435_1, marks a decisive step toward achieving full sovereignty over weapons integration on one of India’s most important combat platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO to Test Submarine-Launched Cruise Missile from Kalvari-Class Subs; 500–800 km Range Planned
item.description: India is preparing to take a major step in underwater strike capability as DRDO plans to conduct live firing trials of its Submarine-Launched Cruise Missile (SLCM) from frontline submarines, likely the Kalvari-class, according to information available with idrw.org. The move follows a successful underwater pontoon test conducted in late 2024, which validated the critical “underwater-to-air” transition and engine ignition sequence.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India to Develop Radar to See Through Plasma Shield of Hypersonic Missiles
item.description: India is advancing a high-priority radar program aimed at one of the most complex challenges in modern air defence: tracking hypersonic targets traveling at speeds exceeding Mach 5. Spearheaded by DRDO’s Electronics and Radar Development Establishment (LRDE), the effort focuses on overcoming the fundamental physics problem posed by the plasma sheath that forms around hypersonic vehicles.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian and German Company to Jointly develop 5-Tonne AeroForce X UAVs
item.description: Aerodata AG and Dynauton (Dynamatic Technologies) have formalized a strategic collaboration to co-develop next-generation unmanned airborne surveillance and reconnaissance systems, centered around the 5-tonne AeroForce X platform. The partnership is geared toward delivering both MALE (Medium Altitude Long Endurance) and HALE (High Altitude Long Endurance) UAV variants tailored for specialized Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) missions across the demanding operational theatres of the Himalayas and the Indian Ocean Region.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tata Elxsi Delivers Critical Fuselage Assembly Jig for HAL’s CATS Warrior UCAV, Paving Way for 2027 First Flight
item.description: Tata Elxsi has delivered a key piece of aerostructures tooling—the center fuselage assembly jig—to Hindustan Aeronautics Limited’s Aircraft Research and Development Centre (ARDC), marking a significant milestone in the development of the CATS Warrior unmanned combat aerial vehicle. The jig will be used to assemble the center fuselage of upcoming flying prototypes, directly supporting HAL’s timeline to achieve a first flight around 2027 for its 2.2-ton loyal wingman platform.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Gridbots develops ‘Humming-Bird’ Throwbot: India’s Compact AI-Enabled Combat Robot for ISR, CBRN Detection and Close-Quarters Operations
item.description: India’s robotics ecosystem is steadily moving into high-impact tactical applications, with Gridbots Technologies unveiling its “Humming-Bird” throwbot—an autonomous, AI-enabled ground robot designed for inspection, surveillance, reconnaissance, and close-quarters combat support. Built as a compact, rugged, and multi-sensor platform, the system reflects a growing shift toward deploying small, intelligent unmanned systems in high-risk environments where human exposure must be minimized.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Army Eyes SH-16 Artillery Upgrade After SH-15 Performance Issues in Recent Operations with India
item.description: The Pakistan Army is reportedly reassessing its artillery modernisation strategy following operational feedback from recent deployments, with a renewed focus on inducting the Chinese SH-16 gun system in both wheeled and tracked configurations. This shift comes despite the earlier induction of the SH-15 Mounted Gun System (MGS), which had been positioned as a key component of Pakistan’s mobile long-range firepower but appears to have delivered mixed results under combat conditions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Germany and India on track to seal submarine deal, says minister
item.description: Germany and India are set to finalize their $8 billion submarine deal within the next three months, according to German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius. Pistorius said Wednesday after talks with his Indian counterpart Rajnath Singh in the northern port city of Kiel that the project involves the production of six submarines in India and that the government in New Delhi was currently coordinating the next steps to seal the deal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy accepts Second Reading acceptance documents (D448) of INS Vagir,
item.description: Second Reading acceptance documents (D448) of INS Vagir, the 5th Kalvari Class Submarine built under Project-75 was signed today by Cdr V Puranik (Retd.), Offg. D(S&HE), MDL and RAdm Arvind Rawal, CSO (Tech), WNC in the presence of senior officials from MDL and the Indian Navy. The signing of the Second Reading acceptance documents (D448) for INS Vagir marks the formal technical handover and acceptance of the submarine by the Indian Navy after a specific period of operational service or a scheduled maintenance intervention.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India and Germany ink Defence Industrial Cooperation Roadmap & Implementing Arrangement for Cooperation in UN Peacekeeping Training
item.description: Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held bilateral talks with Germany’s Federal Minister of Defence Mr Boris Pistorius in Berlin on April 22, 2026 to further bolster the strategic defence partnership with the European nation. The two Ministers discussed a range of bilateral security and defence issues, including priority areas for co-development & co-production of defence equipment, especially in the field of niche technologies. They also reaffirmed the commitment to enhance military-to-military cooperation as a key pillar of the Strategic Partnership.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s IWT Stance Reflects Calibrated Exercise Of Sovereign Rights
item.description: Pakistan’s military establishment treated the sponsorship of terrorism as a low-cost instrument with repercussions largely limited to diplomatic censure, episodic pressure from the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) or restrained military retaliation while the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) continued to function as a “one-way flow of strategic goodwill”, a report highlighted on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: J&K: NIA Attaches Properties Of JeM Operative In 2017 CRPF Camp Attack Case
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) on Wednesday attached several properties belonging to a Jaish-e-Mohammad (JeM) terror operative involved in the 2017 fidayeen attack case on a Central Reserve Police Force camp, in which five paramilitary personnel were killed and three seriously injured.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, China place strategic bets on clean energy out of favour in West
item.description: In the rolling, wind-swept grasslands of Chifeng in northern China’s Inner Mongolia, towering white wind turbines line hilltops like sentinels over a hydrogen industry Beijing is trying to prise away from coal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Manipur: Sophisticated Arms Recovered From Myanmar Border, Illegal Bunkers Dismantled
item.description: Security forces in Manipur have recovered sophisticated arms and ammunition along the India-Myanmar border and, in a separate operation, dismantled six illegal bunkers in the mountainous Kangpokpi district, officials said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Defuses 227-Kg World War II-Era Bomb In Jharkhand, Locals Relieved
item.description: A massive 227-kg bomb dating back to the Second World War, found on the banks of the Subarnarekha river in the Panipora–Nagursai area of the Baharagora block in Jharkhand’s East Singhbhum district, was safely defused by an expert team of the Indian Army on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin
